steps.narrate: The forest thinned. The air cleared — or rather, it replaced one kind of thickness with another.

steps.speaker: Elia
steps.text: You fought through that without burning anything else.

steps.narrate: A single tree, still green, stood at the edge of the clearing like a joke the world had forgotten to finish.
Elia stopped. Her hand went to the bark, fingers spread against the living wood as if checking whether it would answer.
Beyond the treeline, far to the south, the Bureau's tower scraped at the clouds like a finger pointing out an accusation.
That was where the answers were. That was where he would have to go.
But first — the Belt. And the man who might know whether his brother was still alive.
